text: Boyfriend and I went on Halloween night, hoping restaurant would not be packed, and luckily that was the case - they were about 80% full the entire time we were there.  Service was excellent and we felt all the food came out at appropriate times.  They have a huge whiskey selection and a large selection of sodas, some housemade.  Their beer selection, however, is small and odd.  I ordered a Ward 8, one of their whiskey cocktails, and loved it.  Boyfriend was excited to see Busch beer on the menu, until they told him it was in cans, so he ended up just drinking iced tea.  We started with the Biscuits and Breads $8 appetizer, which consisted of one of each of the following: a fluffy biscuit, a flat biscuit, a square of Yankee cornbread, and a wedge of traditional cornbread.  The traditional cornbread was our fav.  You then select 3 of the butters/spreads from the list - we selected the molasses-pecan butter, burnt orange marmalade, and strawberry-blueberry jam - liked them all but the jam was the best.  It was great for 2 people to share, and would also be easy for 3-4 people to share as each person would still get at least 1 bite of everything.  We ordered the whole fried chicken, planning to take some home.  The pieces were large and cooked perfectly.  The breasts are boneless and cut in half.  It is heavily seasoned, which we liked.  They have hot sauce on the table, but we didn't use it.  The whole chicken is expensive at $34, but they are large, antibiotic and hormone free, raised humanely, etc, so I'm not surprised by the price.  We ordered 2 sides - the Jailhouse Cole Slaw and the Mashed Potatoes and Gravy.  We liked them both and they were perfect for 2 people to share.  We did think they were overpriced, based on the serving size.  I'd suggest they increase the serving size a bit or reduce the price.  There are two individual restrooms, both unisex, which is much appreciated by me.  Neither had a mirror though, I'm assuming they just haven't been installed yet.  It was an expensive fried chicken meal - $70 for two people, including one drink, before tip.  As much as we liked it, it will just be a special occasion fried chicken place for us, probably only going once a year, due to the cost.    Things that would have made this a 5-star review:  better beer selection, cheaper sides (maybe cheaper prices in general).

text: Come for the food, tolerate the service.  Muriel's wasn't our first choice, but it was a convenient choice. Wanting to do a fun, french quarter brunch with the family on Sunday, we decided to trek in from Arabi to try and get a spot at Stanley's. Unfortunately, Stanley's does not take reservations and had a 2 hour wait at 1:00 for brunch. Hungry and lacking patience, we sidestepped our way over to Muriel's Jackson Square.  First, let me talk about one of the highlights of our experience. The building itself is fantastico. There are beautiful sky lights that let sunshine just pour into the restaurant. This is a plus. Unsurprisingly, the bar is in a dark, dank corner that was a high-traffic area. Meaning, if you weren't sitting down at the bar, servers were jostling their way by you every few minutes.  The wait caused us to hang out at the dark, dank, not so happy bar area.  Now, hanging out at this bar wouldn't have been so bad if the service were a little better. Ordering drinks for our group proved to be a herculean task. Both my cousin and my wife tried numerous times to get the attention of the two bartenders, one who was working quickly, the other who was working like she could care less that she was there. Time after time, the bartenders would look passed the younger, less wealthy looking women and instead, take the orders of older patrons. My cousin was even on the end of a snide comment by one of the bartenders. Eventually, we received our drinks. The verdict? Good, but not worth the wait.  By the time we got our drinks, almost 45 minutes after sitting at the bar, it was time to take our seats. At this point, the service improved marginally. Our drink guy was friendly and quick to take our drink orders. Our waitress (I'm unsure if she even bothered to introduce herself) was a bit awkward. So what saves this place? The food. The brunch food was damn good. I got the Imperial Crab as an appetizer, which had tons of crab meat and a light breading, and the shrimp and grits as my main. The crab was moist, hot, and just downright tasty. The shrimp and grits were also well above average, with the grits being some of the best grits I have ever had. My wife ordered a bacon/cheese tart type of thing that was also quite good, especially when paired with the fresh fruit. 2-star service is rescued by 4-star food.   The service issues just got more awkward during the bill. We had split the check three ways (that is their limit), so my friend was on my tab with my wife. He put down $40 to cover his meal, and we put the rest on the credit card. Our waitress? Despite the fact that we split our check, she assumed the $40 was the tip for the entire table. We then had to awkwardly tell her that no, that was to be applied toward the bill. We tipped on the card. If I hadn't caught this snafu, we would have paid $40 more than we intended to.   Consensus: I'd go back, but only if I was getting takeout.

text: Well, that's weird. I really feel like I reviewed this Wine Works at some point in the past. Apparently not! All the usual vino suspects are still well-stocked on the shelves, but I headed straight for the beer.  First off: Check the dates. Some of the beers are dusty, and that's a bit disconcerting depending on the style.  Second: Make your own six-pack. I do this whenever possible in general, and they do have a nice selection here. Much larger than Wegmans, smaller than Total Wine, and maybe on par with Canal's, depending on the location.  Also check out their large format bottles. I was surprised to find Transmitter Brewing in stock. Transmitter is a tiny brewery on Long Island City. Like, super tiny. Like, only a few people can comfortably fit in their tasting area at a time. Definitely try their stuff if you see it and like your beer on the funky side.  Check out is always efficient. Obviously be prepared to present your ID if you're anything like me and could pass for a 19-year-old.  Probably the best part of Wine Works? Very reasonable prices. My 6-pack of pretty good stuff came out to $14...aka the price I've paid for one beer on draft on a few occasions.

The servers should know about wine or least have a sommelier (or some one who knows a least a little about wine) speak to us. I asked if there was any wines similar to a Zin and he kept pointing to a Rose???? I repeated multiple time note a white zin. Just a Zinfandel. The food overall was ok. I really enjoyed my meal but could only eat a few bites because I was so frustrated by the entire experience. I had been here before and have recommended it to many other but not anymore. I will just keep going back to places were customers are treated with respect. If you want go food check with amazing service do not come here. I'd recommend Vida (the group is also opening up an Italian restaurant ). So many others places to eat in Indy now with amazing food why come here? Oh and we were celebrating our engagement with my parents for the first time which turned into an unfortunate evening to the point we are celebrating again next weekend to replace last night. I got home and was horrified by my eyebrows. I had a lot raising where she had waxed. Luckily when I called back, I talked to the sweetest girl. text: I purchased a fundraiser voucher for 12 months of small arrangements to support a teacher friend and his school.  For $20, it was a steal, I felt otherwise after my first visit to this florist.  Their hours are only until 5pm on the weekdays, so I made a b-line to their place this past Saturday, realizing I could make it there (barely!) by 3pm to get my very first bouquet.  I called @ 2:40pm to confirm it was ok to pick up and that I would be arriving just at 3pm.  They confirmed to me that yes to come and that we close at 3 as I realized.   I literally pulled in a 2:59pm and the woman was out in the lot, putting out their closed sign when I parked.  I confirmed it was still ok for me to come in and I barely got a response.  She led me to the counter, had me complete the form, told me a couple things that I could clearly understand on the voucher and handed me my dainty bouquet of flowers.  Mostly small roses (4 of which will never open), but a nice little surprise for my 8 year old daughter who loves getting flowers. I realize I pushed the limits by showing up when they close, but I called first to get confirmation it was ok, advised I'd be getting there at the very last minute and I felt like they could not spare an extra couple minutes to possibly appreciate a potential new customer.  Oh well, I expect too much from businesses.  I will hope for a better experience when I arrive in the middle of their work day.  If not, I will get my $ worth for what I spent and spend my hard earned money somewhere else.  It's as simple as that.  Sorry to say I cannot add anything else to Yelp b/c I was not given any extra time to take a peek around or check out prices, etc....  **Revised to add a note that the owner was not the one who helped me on this day and they generously sent me a gift certificate with an apology which I find very classy, so I am headed back to check them out real soon**

query: friendly staff cafe
text: A good place and it certainly has its high points but it also has some weaknesses that  detract. An excellent selection of wines by the glass at very fair prices; they really know what they are doing here. Menu is pretty trendy and not overly creative, in step with what other hip restaurants are doing these days. But execution falls short sometimes. To wit, a bouillabaisse that was way, way over-salted and included three of five un-opened mussels. But, I suppose, in fairness, ordering seafood in the middle of a desert.... A vegetarian plate called Summer Vegetables centered on a described five-onion risotto with blue cheese. But a warm-spice seasoning -- five spice powder? maybe something North African? not sure -- simply failed. It didn't work with the blue cheese at all, and I wouldn't be willing to testify under oath that I could taste any onions (one of my favorite things). Still, grilled baby zucchini were deeply infused with rich, maybe mesquite, smoke and were sublime. Possibly the best thing I've tasted all summer, and I'm usually bored by zucchini. An ordinary mesclun salad was fresh and tasty, dressed lightly; a slab of heirloom tomato was nice; fresh little French green beans were cooked perfectly. A corn coulis was pleasant; radicchio was there as advertised, just not much of it, maybe a few scraps. I wanted to like the dish more, and I would have but for that dissonant risotto. Others in our party enjoyed their meat-based entrees which are perhaps a more likely bet in the Southwest. Service was good, staff welcoming and friendly. Decor is evocative of an old rail dining car without being goofy, but rather quite nicely stylish.

I got the Chicken Shish Tawook platter with a side of hummus and grape leaves.   Chicken was cubed, chewy and lacking spices.... as was the rice it was laying on. Hummus? I was hoping the garlic sauce would help me ignore the frustration that was my platter......until I realized the garlic sauce was made with mayo. MAYO, people!! I nearly cried. What a freakin' sin.  The best thing on my plate you ask? The 2 little grape leaves sadly sitting atop a small garden salad. They were pretty lackluster, but after the atrocity that was the rest of my platter...my palate welcomed a little flavor.  The small salad was served with a container of generic oil/vinegar dressing. query: friendly staff cafe
text: Celebrated a post Phillies victory here last week. I hadn't been in the Rittenhouse Square district in a while, so I asked my friend if we could eat in the area.   The place was a lot smaller than I imagined, but I loved the ambiance of the bar/restaurant- small booths and high top tables, the subway tile, the old school looking bar.   Our server was very friendly and started us right away with plenty of water. It was amazingly hot that day, so we both passed on drinks. We had taken a look at the menu while at the game, and we were intrigued by their pickle menu. Our server suggested the turnips and kale, and we decided to go with that. The mason jar full of pickled turnips and kale, served with creamy ricotta and olive tapenade was just what we needed, since we didn't have a big meal during the game (and we were pretty hungry). The ricotta spread was creamy, and though I am not a huge fan of olives (i avoid them usually) the tapenade was soooooo good- briny and just enough olive, but smooth without being too overpowering.   My friend got the veggie burger, and I got a regular burger and we split an order of their duck fat fries. The burgers came out promptly and I loved that mine was this perfectly shaped patty, cooked to order, and I loved the thousand island dressing as the condiment. My friend's massive veggie burger looked good too- it was topped with tons of avocado. The fries were soooo good too- crisp and salty, thick and substantial, without being too greasy. We ate the last crumb!  Our bill was reasonable, considering we didn't have any alcohol- 35 bucks. I definitely will return on a future trip to Philly, perhaps for fancy drinks.

text: My husband and I decided to stay a night in our own city to celebrate our anniversary.  We usually don't take time to see the sights locally.  We chose the Hotel Indigo for its location, modern decor and we liked the fact it is a renovated older building.  It really was a disappointment.  Upon check in we were "greeted" by a nice, but ambivalent, desk clerk.  We were asked if we used valet parking.  We told him we did not.  He asked me to initial a form stating we parked ourselves, which I did.  We were checked into a wheelchair accessible room, which was fine but not necessary.  The room was very clean and well appointed.  But the shower had no door or curtain.  I thought they certainly designed it so that water would not end up all over the floor.  Wrong!  Despite the fact I tried everything I could to keep water from going everywhere, I still ended up with and inch or more of water ALL over the floor.  I used every clean towel (save for 1 to dry off with) to mop it up.  Not the end of the world, but very very irritating.  After getting ready, I attempted to call the front desk to ask for more towels so my husband could take a shower in the morning.  No answer...after calling several times.  We decided to ask the front desk clerk in person for more towels as we left for the evening.  We went downstairs and I told the same clerk who checked us in about the disaster of a shower.  He nodded as if he'd heard this problem many times before.  He told us he would contact someone to bring more towels up while we were out for the evening.  We went out for several hours and came back to the hotel.  I went into the room and straight to the bathroom to see if towels were brought as promised.  They were not!  And the soppy wet towels were still piled on the floor (no place to hang towels anywhere in the bathroom).  Oh...and when I took my shower earlier it was lukewarm at best.  NO hot water.  I didn't bother calling the front desk again (about the towels) since i was sure nobody would answer and my husband had to skip his morning shower.  Other irritations were the TV never worked properly and the wi-fi was very slow.  In the morning we noticed the bill had been slipped under the door and we took a look at it.  Even though we hadn't used valet, we had been charged $28 for valet parking.  Great.  When we checked out, the front desk clerk (a woman this time) actually thought we were lying about the valet.  I told her I had  initialed a document stating we didn't use valet and she obviously didn't believe we had done that.  After some convincing, we finally did get a credit.  But to make a guest feel like a liar...especially after the service we'd gotten since checking in....was ridiculous.  We won't be back. Pros:  Good location, clean rooms, nice toiletries (Aveda products), modern decor, nice linens Cons:  Terrible customer service, no shower doors or curtains in wheelchair accessible rooms, slow wi-fi, no hot water This hotel could be great.  What a disapointment.

text: The food is awesome,  huge side portions and wraps. My friend and I picked the first 2 wraps on the board to share. Layout and where you have to go to pay is a little confusing, but the staff is nice and helped us new-bees. Tall ceilings and open kitchen concept in a auto garage shell is fine with us. Walls were decorated in awesome graffiti art, and we enjoyed looking at the album covered tables, games, and local art. Staff had an eclectic look and were well trained- didn't act like corporate robots. Which is always a plus. I didn't see this place as "a wanna be hipster place", just a relaxed restaurant were anyone can come and enjoy good food. I've watched suit and tie guys, military in uniforms,  hippies,  college students, kids with parents and everyone in-between come in and eat.   Bathroom was clean and had plenty of back up toilet paper and paper towels in a basket.   Overall great food, great atmosphere, good music, awesome staff. So glad we stopped at Phat Bites to eat. This area needed a place like this.

text: Steamboats are legendary as are the Dukes of Dixieland, and on the Natchez river cruises, you get to enjoy both!  The Dukes have been famous for over 50 years with their traditional dixieland jazz music.  And steamboats were at the heart of the growth of our nation through the 19th century.  If you are in New Orleans in the Summer, I recommend the evening dinner cruise.  If it is any other season, the afternoon lunch cruise would be better due to the loss of sunlight in the evening.  (You would end up photographing white pin-pricks from scattered lights in a sea of darkness.)  On the other hand, the Dukes of Dixieland play on every cruise, so you can't go wrong no matter which cruise you choose.  The meals are served buffet-style, and it is "all you can eat".  The extra 10 or 12  bucks for lunch is definitely worthwhile.  The most popular item for "seconds" (and "thirds") was the bread pudding with bourbon sauce.  An extra $25 for dinner?  I dunno ... the food is good, but not great.  A lunch cruise is the best value IMHO, but a sunset cruise with the city lights just turning on is a photographer's dream ("golden time").

text: Love Sabino Canyon and the $20 annual pass is so worth it, even if you're just going to be in town for a week or so and just want to get your hike on. So many different trails and difficulties...if you just want to do a short walk, you can take a stroll up the main road. Or, do a longer hike, like up to Seven Falls.   Also, just a word of warning if you aren't familiar with hiking in the desert: it's a whole different ballgame than what you're used to! I went through over 100 oz of water hiking for about 3.5 hours/9 miles or so from parking lot to Seven Falls, and I easily could have drank more. To compare, I typically go through 64 oz on an 8 hour/12 mile hike with 4,000 ft of elevation gain in the mountains outside of LA. So, in summary: be prepared and pack more than what you think you'll need, especially if you aren't familiar with the desert! Listen to your body and if you need to stop, turn around!

text: This place is certainly a pretty long haul from Nashville proper.  I grew up in Hendersonville, and Gateway was the place I always took my truck for full service check ups--oil changes, tire rotations, all that good stuff.  My dad really likes the place and trusts the guys who work there, so I'll still occasionally take my vehicle out there for service.  Gateway offers pretty substantial discounts for teachers and their families.  My mom teaches fourth grade, so our family always got the educator's discount.  The fellas that work at the service desk in Gateway are always straight-forward regarding how long you'll have to wait.  Let's be honest...getting your vehicle serviced rarely costs you less than an hour of wait time.  But in my experience, Gateway at least gives you a pretty accurate estimate of how long you'll be waiting around.  As far as the actual work Gateway does, I've never experienced any problems.  If you're like me, however, you wouldn't have any clue if they didn't do a damn thing.  I seriously doubt that, however, considering how honest the guys that run the place seem.  Having your vehicle serviced isn't glamorous and it certainly isn't fun, but you can bank on this place to do it pretty quickly and cheaply.

text: My wife and I visit New Orleans regularly, and every time we're there, we go to Lüke every day for Happy Hour. Their oysters are stellar, and at half price, I eat four dozen at a sitting. We have tried quite a few of the items on their menu, and my wife recently had their Ragout of Wild Boar and loved it. I tried it too, and it was a fantastic treat. Their Crab Bisque rocks, and it's a decent sized portion. We've also had the Flamenkuchen, which is like a flatbread pizza with bacon and onion; it's quite tasty and crispy. We've had their giant Lüke burgers and loved them too, they're fantastic, and prepared perfectly. In fact, we brought their burgers back on the plane last year, and they were delectable. They serve a bevy of beers, whiskeys, cognacs, wines and other spirits, all of high quality and drinkability. I actually drink the Mountain Valley Sparkling Water most of the time, with an occasional beer or wine, which are also half price for Happy Hour. The servers here are great, and we drew one of the same friendly waiters, Kyle P., that we had last year, for a couple of our visits this year. He was exceptional and warm, as well as genuine. The service is fast,  but not rushed, and the manner is warm and professional. The atmosphere is upscale, but not pretentious or haughty, and the dress is from business, to t-shirt-casual. The prices are not cheap, but the food is outstanding. Owner John Besh runs a tight ship, and we owe our lovely friend Marci for turning us on to their Happy Hour. Thank you Lüke for making our afternoons between 3 and 6pm in New Orleans, oyster-packed, comfort-food-filled and very enjoyable.

text: The food here was very good, but there are certainly some imperfections.  DOWNSIDES:  First off, keep in mind that this place is far more American than Japanese. Creative nonetheless, but it is far from authentic. There is a lot of grease and frying invoked.  The big downside to this establishment is the pricing. Some dishes were ridiculously small and simple for the price. Granted, this is a kind of tapas restaurant, but some of the dishes should be taken off the menu if they're going to charge these prices. There was a dish with a few cubes beets, and it was five dollars. They just tasted like beets, nothing more. Very disappointing. This was only one of the many overly simple dishes that are $5+  The service was also rather slow. It took 10 minutes for each small plate to arrive. In addition to this, the music was extremely loud and distracting, and the lighting was much too dim.  Another downside to this place was the lack of vegetarian options. Everything either had pieces of pork belly, or was fried in duck fat and whatnot.   UPSIDES:  But I will say that their vegetarian ramen was out of this world. It was kind of misleading though because there was no broth; it was more of a thick coconut curry with noodles. Extremely tasty nonetheless.  The Spicy Shrimp Gyoza (dumplings) was also incredible. They were very well seasoned and flavorful.  All in all, we spent around $120 for a party of three, and were still a bit hungry. But if you have the money, the food here is very good. We might be back for a special occasion.

query: best pizza in town
text: With all of the great restaurants in NOLA, there is no need to stop at this one. That was a $15 entrée....no side, no nothing.  Pass on by this one!

text: Atmosphere - Pretty intimate BYOB. They even have an area where you can sit on the floor and eat, though it was empty when we were there.  Service - As good as you can ask for. text: I've always bemoaned the lack of good hot dog places nearby. No more. Hot Diggity has great dogs and fresh cut fries. I had the Saigon which is like a banh mi but with a hot dog instead of compressed pig's ears. Come to think of it that may not be all that much different. The greens on the Saigon were super crisp and fresh and the fries were awesome. They could use less salt though.  A few suggestions:  1) They have dogs from all over the world but no Philly dog? 2) When you walk in the first thing you see is the posting for the Bronx Bomber. Now I'm from New York originally but I'd rather not see a dog named for the NY Yankees right when I walk into the place, in Philly. 3) The menu drawings for each item are cool but you can't see the ones in the back. Hopefully they'll add a menu on the wall to the left to look at if you are in line. 4) They put aluminum foil on the grill to cook the dogs. Heresy! I grew up near the original Nathan's. One of the reasons Nathan's hot dogs taste so good is that the grill gets seasoned with all the dogs cooking all day. I know the foil thing is cleaner but seriously, if I was that interested in cleanliness why would I be eating hot dogs in the first place?  Overall though this is my new go to place. Service is friendly and food is great. Good ambience. I hope they don't ruin it with a TV like so many other places have.

text: I get the Retrospect thing.  I know it's not necessarily a thrift store - it's a business that does the work of seeking out the best thrift store finds for you.  Thus the price gouging.  I'll admit it - sometimes I don't feel like spending two hours just to find the perfect new vintage dress.  Lately I've been pretty nonplussed by their selection, though I imagine things have a quick turnaround there on South Street.  The shoe selection is honestly what keeps me coming back - I've found some wonderful boots there.  They also have some beautiful vintage furniture - and for some of the gigantic couches they've put on sale, the prices aren't too bad.  Retrospect isn't a necessity, but for the few times a month I'm down on South Street, curiosity will still continue to draw me in.

text: The food ranks 3 stars but the service ranks only 1/2 star, dragging the over-all star rating down to a sub-zero number. "Food!" I answered. Yep. Food.   She continued on with what she was doing: the grueling task of flipping tortillas on a grill. I full expected the woman's head to twist a complete 360 degrees, a trick I once saw in the movie "The Exorcist". text: Where do I start?!? I visited with a friend in January when it was still glam holiday decor and it was just stunning.   I did make a reservation online, which is an awesome feature. I did notice when we left that there were many people up front waiting for a table. I do think that in such a beautiful location and an establishment that offers drinks, food, and the primary focus of desserts it is smarter to plan ahead and reserve your spot.   My friend and I started with the white wedding tea which came by recommendation from our server (in a really cutesy uniform) in a gorgeous teapot and it was perfect for sharing. I'd say that we both were able to get about 2 or 3 cups each. The tea itself was surprisingly delicious. I'm more of a coffee drinker, but the bakery really makes you feel like you're there for a tea party so you feel inclined to partake - haha!  Additionally, we both got the pink passion sparkling which was also fantastic and garnished with a rose petal which makes it feel extra special.   For lunch, I had the jam & rosemary quiche and arugula salad which was delicious but I was definitely having envy over my friends three cheese cavatappi - basically a grown up, fancy macaroni and cheese and will 100% be my order next time.   For dessert, I had the raspberry champagne cake and she ordered the red velvet. No envy there, because my piece was incredible. Seriously, I'm still dreaming about when I'll have my next one. I took my husband home a piece of Gwendolyn's famous earl's court chocolate cake. I can't say how that tasted because I swear I didn't take a bite before I gave it to him (it was really good).   Because we simply couldn't be stopped, we also each had a rose macaron and that was weird. I've never had a macaron and I've never eaten a rose before, but it tasted like bubblegum and was really chewy. It seemed like it was right and I could be tempted to have another.   Overall, it was a 10/10 experience. I would highly recommend visiting. It isn't necessarily just the cake that makes your visit, it is a true experience. I've kept up with the Instagram account and the thought and time she puts into the decor is so special and you can feel the heart in The Cake Bake Shop.    (PS even the salt is pink)

text: Boyfriend and I went on Halloween night, hoping restaurant would not be packed, and luckily that was the case - they were about 80% full the entire time we were there.  Service was excellent and we felt all the food came out at appropriate times.  They have a huge whiskey selection and a large selection of sodas, some housemade.  Their beer selection, however, is small and odd.  I ordered a Ward 8, one of their whiskey cocktails, and loved it.  Boyfriend was excited to see Busch beer on the menu, until they told him it was in cans, so he ended up just drinking iced tea.  We started with the Biscuits and Breads $8 appetizer, which consisted of one of each of the following: a fluffy biscuit, a flat biscuit, a square of Yankee cornbread, and a wedge of traditional cornbread.  The traditional cornbread was our fav.  You then select 3 of the butters/spreads from the list - we selected the molasses-pecan butter, burnt orange marmalade, and strawberry-blueberry jam - liked them all but the jam was the best.  It was great for 2 people to share, and would also be easy for 3-4 people to share as each person would still get at least 1 bite of everything.  We ordered the whole fried chicken, planning to take some home.  The pieces were large and cooked perfectly.  The breasts are boneless and cut in half.  It is heavily seasoned, which we liked.  They have hot sauce on the table, but we didn't use it.  The whole chicken is expensive at $34, but they are large, antibiotic and hormone free, raised humanely, etc, so I'm not surprised by the price.  We ordered 2 sides - the Jailhouse Cole Slaw and the Mashed Potatoes and Gravy.  We liked them both and they were perfect for 2 people to share.  We did think they were overpriced, based on the serving size.  I'd suggest they increase the serving size a bit or reduce the price.  There are two individual restrooms, both unisex, which is much appreciated by me.  Neither had a mirror though, I'm assuming they just haven't been installed yet.  It was an expensive fried chicken meal - $70 for two people, including one drink, before tip.  As much as we liked it, it will just be a special occasion fried chicken place for us, probably only going once a year, due to the cost.    Things that would have made this a 5-star review:  better beer selection, cheaper sides (maybe cheaper prices in general).

query: I am looking for a cheap sushi restaurant with fresh ingredients
text: The boyfriend and I went to Samurai Sushi on a Friday night with high hopes as it is a place with 140 reviews that averaged 4 stars.  We learned that Nashville doesn't have a really great sushi restaurant to put bring this very average restaurant back down to earth.  We recently moved from Orlando so with our first paychecks we thought it'd be adorable to treat ourselves/celebrate new beginnings, etc etc etc.  6 rolls of sushi, a handful of sashimi, and about $70+ later, we were left with the overwhelming feeling of meeehhh.  Firstly, the fish isn't all that fresh and the rolls are over-done.  Whatever happened to just letting the integrity of the ingredients speak for itself?  It's freaking sushi. You don't need to put 10 different things into a roll and then deep fry it and then cover it in eel sauce for it to be delicious.  Some good quality fish and properly prepared rice is really all you need.  Let the quality of the fish be the star of the roll and for the love of god stop deep frying everything.  Generations of Japanese are rolling in their graves.  And what is with this whole strawberry sushi craze?  I thought I'd give it a shot based on Yelp recommendations and 2 weeks on, my stomach still churns at the memory.  It's not that Samurai is the absolute worst sushi in Nashville (you can go to Ken's for that), it's just that the food as a whole is really really mehhh.  I don't know if it's because TN has to source its fish from the coast and as a result all of its sushi restaurants suck or if its because Samurai just has a really cheap and therefore crappy fish supplier.  Either way, we wont' be back to Samurai.  Pros:  1. the place is cheap.  3 "fancy" rolls, 3 normal rolls, about 8 pieces of sashimi, and 2 beers was about $75ish with tip.   2. the restaurant is also cleaner than Ken's though that particular standard of cleanliness isn't really tough to beat. 3. most of the food isn't absolutely disgusting.  Cons: 1. the rolls consist of more ingredients than a Papa John's supreme pizza 2. the fish isn't really like that fresh. 3. overall just very average food.

text: Pags rules this street with three separate venues: a pizza place, a fancier Italian restaurant, and the super-romantic wine bar we love.  The front room looks like an old time train station waiting area, with its dramatic hanging lights, banquettes, and tiny tables (AND the Bridgette Bardot poster).    The wine menu is heavily French and not cheap, so savor those glasses, but they will give you a taste before you invest.  For example, they had a Veuve Cliquot Yellow Label open to try by the glass ($13, and a full bottle was $84).   Most wine places don't have such an expensive bottle available for glasses.  The last time we went, we were in the mood for rosé, having just accomplished one of life's milestones.  Due to excellent recommendations from the owner, we developed a major crush on Pommery champagne, which they had as splits in two different versions, pink and blue.  Oh, such a cute half-size wine bucket too.  There is a full bar, not just wine, and sometimes they bring out free tapas such as kalamata olives (plus a little dish for the pits, nice touch).  We have enjoyed the arugula pizza back in our ovo-lacto days, but you don't come here for the food.  The building is long and narrow, so the back part is even darker and more intimate, if you need to hide out, and there is a tiny patio in front though I have never seen anyone on it.  Music can be anything from classic jazz to electronica, and I don't remember any tvs, hurray.  Recently, Nina Simone was playing, just perfect for our mood.  My bf got into a discussion about Indian and Brazilian music with the owner, who then played some recommended Brazilian artists from his own collection for us and wrote down the names so we could hunt them down later. The camera I purchased was about $60 more at Schiller's than on the internet but the customer service was totally worth it for me. The extra battery and memory card put the price a bit above what I wanted to spend but then Brad told me since I bought the camera there, I get 25 free prints every month for a year. That was totally worth spending the extra dough on an extra battery and then he even threw in a little camera case! What a guy!   Schiller's seems to have a great selection of cameras and camera equipment. The one gentleman that helped me for a minute was a little awkward to deal with and my friend said he had a little bit of difficulty with customer service. Brad and the guy who helped me with my camcorder were great to deal with so I guess it all depends on which employee is helping you. I definitely recommend giving Schiller's a chance. query: The service at this restaurant was really bad and disappointing
text: Oh, where to start? My dear friends, it's Christmas 2013 and I begrudgingly trekked from my beautiful, frozen home in Chicago to the busted, desolate wasteland of St. Louis, MO on a layover on my way home. A friend--who works for the company--got me a massive cut on a room rate. I, being an opportunist, couldn't possibly pass up the chance to stay the night for mere pennies.   Probably should've thought twice on that one.   My friend made the reservation and paid for it as a gift to me for the holiday. She told me everything had been taken care of and the manager for the evening shift--I wasn't getting in until 12:30 that night--would have everything ready for me to go when I arrived. I roll in, exhausted, and am greeted by one of St. Louis's best. Best what, I'm not sure, because she definitely had more attitude than any one person should be allowed to have that late at night.   Turns out, everything had *not* been taken care of and she would need to see the purchasing credit card and ID for the buyer. Naturally, my friend is miles and miles away. I explain to her that everything had been taken care of already and that I was told I would only need to check in. With a roll in her eye and a blank stare of contempt, she works with me to figure out a way to get me checked in.   On surface value, the room was fine. All the lamps worked, the sheets were comfy and the bed was somewhere in between lying on top of a blanket on the floor, and falling asleep on your yoga mat. Notably, there were four pillows. Four. As a man who loves a multitude of pillows, I was thrilled to see this.... until I crawled into bed and realized they were European-style square pillows. Square. Four squares don't make a right, Ramada.   But! Oh, but... as I tried to drift off to sleep, I remembered... "Come on down for breakfast tomorrow. We serve between 9 and 11." I thought... why, yes. I will come down for breakfast, thank you. And so I did. I showered, trying to ignore the plastic PVC piping hanging, visible underneath the sink--held together entirely by duct tape, I might add--and I trotted my happy self on down to get a muffin. "On the other side of the bar," I remembered Miss Attitude 2012 saying.   A nice, small dining area. Friendly server. This, I thought, THIS is what I was expecting. Ah, yes. Mmmm... soupy eggs! Dry, stale breakfast potatoes. Gravy for my biscuits that resembled milk with brown chunks in it. Muffins! Muffins with tops so dry and old Moses himself must've baked them. Cream cheese in tiny cartons, sitting out, unrefrigerated, uniced, and room temperature. Yum!  I get up to leave, thinking, "Who cares that much? It's a hotel breakfast." And then the check came. I looked at it, it looked at me. I looked at my server, he looked at my wallet.   "Wha... what?"   Ah yes, my darling Yelpers. Not a free breakfast! Oh no. Ten dollars for all you can eat of food you wouldn't choose unless you absolutely had to. And even then, you might give pause.   Just... don't. Ok? Just stay away. Unless you like going to hotels with busted elevators, Real Housewives blaring in the lobby and Fox News in the "restaurant." Don't stay here unless you enjoy your bedroom window looking out onto a shared atrium with a pool where loud children will be screaming at 9 in the morning. Don't stay here unless you want hidden parking fees and misleading promises of breakfast. If you sleep on a yoga mat with a couch pillow and enjoy looking at your plumbing while washing your hands in a leaky faucet... well, I guess Ramada can help.

It was 9:40pm and we had to walk around downtown I day to search for a place that would serve the girls food. As expected nothing worked so we had to resort to ordering from a pizza place. By the time they ate it was 11:00pm at night. I later found out another team in our program placed a delivery order from them the evening before. The food arrived 2.5 hours late and was missing a lot of what was ordered. In my opinion this Giordano's should be out of business. The fact that they cancelled my order and never called is completely unacceptable!! I'm still waiting for my refund that they claim they issued. query: The service at this restaurant was really bad and disappointing
text: Today was my first experience with VSEC (and hopefully last, for the health of my pets!)  My cat who has been vomitting for several days needed an abdominal ultrasound.    The facility is easy to fine, right off of Bus. Rt. 1 in Levittown.  When I came in the main entrance, I noticed the waiting area was rather large, and borken off into several areas. One area had a TV and several groups of people and their pets, and was rather loud.  After checking in, I found a nice quiet corner for me and my cat to sit, away from all of the noise which would have added to the stressful experience.  Near where I sat, I noticed a nice little area where you could make yourself coffee in a Keurig.  There was also a sign with the wi-fi log in information, which was nice to pass the time and take my mind off my stress.    My appt. was at 2:30.  At about 2:35 a nurse came out and sat with me and went over my cat's history.  She explained what would be involved in performing the ultrasound, and what would happen from there.  She took my cat back (I hate when they take the cat and not me, also, but I understand).  About 25 minutes or so later she returned with my cat, in the carrier, and walked me back to one of the exam rooms.  She told me how my cat behaved very well, and explained that the doctor would be in shortly, as soon as he finished typing up his report, to tell me his findings.  About a minute later, Dr. MacLeod entered, gave me a copy of the report, and told me the news - thankfully, all good.  By that point he had already talked on the phone with my regular vet and was faxing her the report.    For a large specialty hospital, with lots of patients coming and going while I was there, I was impressed with the punctuality of my appointment.  I was out of there an hour after my appointment time, which I didn't think was too bad.  The facility is a little far for me, and in an emergency I'd be more likely to go to my local emergency vet office, but this is who my vet referred me to for the ultrasound, and I was pleased with their treatment of me and my cat.

text: This place is a gem! Look no further..  I just moved back to St. Louis after being in Manhattan for 3 years. While out running errands, I decided to get a mani on the way home. I Yelped and this place got great reviews so I decided to walk in. Immediately, I was greeted by a women in a beautiful pink suit who fit me in right away without an appointment (an appointment was recommended on Yelp). She took me over to the color selection bar and while I was set on getting a gel shellac mani, she told me the powder gel dip mani was much better in terms of quality and duration to last. I was open to trying so picked my color (the selection is unreal - they have EVERY color and more!) and sat down. She then offered me a beverage. The water she brought out had delicious freshly cut oranges in it. What a nice touch!   My manicurist was Jan who was so nice and conversant. Her attention to detail was fantastic and she made sure I loved my nails, especially being the first time with the powder dip. Toward the end, the nice woman who greeted me coming in (I am guessing a manager) came over and sat down to make sure everything was to my liking and was quite pleasant to converse with as well. Once finished, I was in love with my nails - the best I've ever had.   As I was paying, a gentleman seated by the counter asked to see me nails. I thought he was another customer or waiting for his wife but it turns out he was the owner! I told him of my fantastic experience and how well run his salon was. He was very customer centric and could tell he knew exactly how to run a successful business. That goes such a long way and hard to find (especially when you are used to NYC nail salons where customer service is nonexistent and it's all about the dollar.)  Also, this place is gorgeous, spacious and clean - by far the nicest salon I've been to. The Christmas decorations were top notch inside and out.   I gave Bello 4 starts because it is definitely pricey. $42 (excluding tip) for a powder dip manicure. The most I've ever paid for a gel manicure in $25. But despite the price, the service and experience was the best I've ever had (and I've been to hundreds of nail salons in different cities!) I will be going back in a few weeks and look forward to trying a pedicure at some point!  If you don't mind paying top dollar to be completely pampered, this is your place!

The Swedish massage was relaxing but I wouldn't say it really massaged too many muscles. I literally felt like I was getting a nice rub all over my body. My mother said her massage was good but it became painful when her masseuse used her elbow to massage her shoulder blade. Now that we know, the next time we come back we'll request the deep tissue massage but medium pressure.   Thanks to their promotion on yelp, we were able to save 10% on our services. I was able to check in but my mother wasn't able to see the 10% discount for checking in. We even tried multiple devices but the kind receptionist eventually gave us the discount anyways.   Note: The discount is per person so we both had to check in to get a complete 10% off our bill since we paid together.   We will definitely be back for future services.

text: I love Italian food, and I already have my favorite places around Nashville we frequently visit (Mama Mia's and Molinari's). I thought this place was a chain (since there is one near my work in Nashville and the one in Cool Springs near our house). But apparently it is a local joint with two locations, so my expectations suddenly became higher.   The ambience was kinda weird. It was noisy and somewhat trendy by looks, but small as far as overall seating. I wouldn't come here for an intimate date night. Our service was sub par. Our waitress was slow to get our order, my husband's side salad came before my wine, and we never received any bread with our meals (we noticed everyone else received bread at their table).   The food was good, but not great. The pasta is homemade and the sauces were good, but there were not a lot of choices as far as sauces. I typically would like an olive oil, pesto, or butter/white wine sauce choice to choose from, but most were tomato based (marinara or tomato cream). While I love tomatoes, I just didn't feel the options were diverse. But I will say that they have several entree and pizza options as well.  We may or may not go back. They'll have one chance to improve their service if I do. And I will probably try their wood fired pizza next time.

text: Texas Roadhouse is a throw peanut shells on the floor style of steak house. There's a bunch of steak house chains that say it's cool to throw peanut shells on the floor. query: This place has the best pizza I have ever tried in town
text: This place is certainly a pretty long haul from Nashville proper.  I grew up in Hendersonville, and Gateway was the place I always took my truck for full service check ups--oil changes, tire rotations, all that good stuff.  My dad really likes the place and trusts the guys who work there, so I'll still occasionally take my vehicle out there for service.  Gateway offers pretty substantial discounts for teachers and their families.  My mom teaches fourth grade, so our family always got the educator's discount.  The fellas that work at the service desk in Gateway are always straight-forward regarding how long you'll have to wait.  Let's be honest...getting your vehicle serviced rarely costs you less than an hour of wait time.  But in my experience, Gateway at least gives you a pretty accurate estimate of how long you'll be waiting around.  As far as the actual work Gateway does, I've never experienced any problems.  If you're like me, however, you wouldn't have any clue if they didn't do a damn thing.  I seriously doubt that, however, considering how honest the guys that run the place seem.  Having your vehicle serviced isn't glamorous and it certainly isn't fun, but you can bank on this place to do it pretty quickly and cheaply.

query: This place has the best pizza I have ever tried in town
text: Oh how I love Flying Pie - and how exciting that there is now a Meridian location! But I have to agree, it was not the Flying Pie experience I have come to know and love. There's also an omlette station that makes a very good omelette.    Seating is available inside, or outside in front, and I believe there's a pool-side area as well  Gripes.  Well, parking downtown St Pete sucks no matter where you are at.  But you typically find yourself along 4th Ave N, curbside.  Watch the traffic, you can get plowed getting out on the traffic-side.    Mainly my issue has been coffee refills.  I'm not an uppity reviewer, but there are just basic things that should go without saying.  And coffee refills is one of them.  Nearly every time we've eaten here, it's damn near impossible to get a refill.  You ask, but then you're lucky if anyone comes back.  And if they do, it's a way beyond acceptable time frame.  This morning again, I drained my original cup, and our server never came by to check.  I had to ask.  Then the Server never came back.  Then I had to ask the busboy.  This is typical, we usually have to find someone other than our server to get the coffee refilled.  And, while waiting on the busboy, we watched our Server go by several times.  No coffee.  Our busboy was back in a flash with coffee.  When it came time to pay, our Server wasted no time.  She was there in a heartbeat and back again to collect our cards and go run our checks.  Where was this speed on the coffee?  It's painfully clear no one wants you to sit and enjoy yourselves for very long.  You are being rushed to leave to open the table for others.  I get it, they work on tips.  But that's also not good service to pull this kind of stunt.  And it means you're getting less of a tip.  I mean, I'm STILL EATING.  I shouldn't have to go for more than a few minutes with all the Servers running by.

query: This place has the best pizza I have ever tried in town
text: Finally made it here last night after a year of hearing how great it is and we weren't disappointed!  Now as some of the other reviewers have stated, this is a true dive bar, not the cutesy, intentional dive bar, but all out duct taped booths, graffiti covered bathroom, "we ain't doing anything here unless the roof is collapsing" dive bar - which I actually kind of like:)  I didn't have any interactions with the employees other than showing my ID to get in, but the husband never complained when he came back from ordering.    They are definitely a no nonsense kinda place.  We were told, "Menu's on the table, head up to the bar when you are ready to order."  But luckily we were able to snag a booth right away and didn't have the stress of table stalking and waiting for someone to leave in order to sit down.  We waited about 45 minutes for our pizza, which was fine because it's what we expected going in, so we just chatted with our friends and enjoyed a pitcher of beer while we waited.    We ended up sharing a medium (12-inch?) Chicago style with peps and mushrooms and I pretty much fell out of the booth when I saw the cheese oozing out - this was my kind of pizza!  Now if you are all about the sauce and toppings I would not recommend this because the bread and cheese are the obvious standouts.  I was a little worried that with all the cheese the crust would be soggy but it was awesomely crisp on the bottom and probably sturdy enough to pick up had I wanted to.  The sauce was deliciously spicy and we guessed might have had sun dried toms in it to give it the deeper hue?  After one slice I was full, after another half slice I was woozy, this pizza is no joke!    So now I can finally say we have been to Black Thorn and I will happily return the next time a pizza craving or urge to eat a pound of cheese in one sitting strikes.  Thank you for the fun night out!

text: I chose Convivio for our book club gathering at the recommendation of a friend.  At the end of the year, we vote on the favorite restaurant we visited for the year and the winner gets a prize.  In over a decade, I have only won this coveted prize once.  Maybe this year will be my second!  When we arrived, we were directed to a long rectangle table for the Seven of us, but when a member Spotted a round table off to the side and asked if we could sit there instead, our waiter was happy to oblige.  Round tables are so much better for discussion!  It's a good thing we did move because as the evening progressed, it got a little loud.  We wouldn't have been able to hear each other at all at the other table.   I, honestly, expected this to be a quiet restaurant and want to visit again to see if the noise level that evening is the norm or if we just had a couple of loud groups nearby.    Not a single food order was duplicated and we were still all satisfied with our orders.   I ordered the John Dory fish dish which came with a sweet potato purée and Brussels sprouts.  It was so good!!   Evidently John Dory is a rarity that people come in for specifically when they have it.   I almost didn't order it because our waiter (when I asked if it was) confirmed that it is, indeed, the fish in Finding Nemo named Dory.  My friends convinced me to get it anyway, but I didn't eat it without at least minimal emotional trauma.  When I got home and googled it, I found that it was not Dory and felt better about eating the deliciousness I was served!    At the end of our meal our check was delivered....in a book!!  How much more perfect can you get for group of book lovers?     As I said, I will definitely come back and will definitely recommend to others.

query: This place has the best pizza I have ever tried in town
text: THANK YOU FOR RUINING MY CHRISTMAS DINNER. Huge rip off $75 per person prefixed (the worst) Christmas dinner ever.  Had Christmas dinner with my husband today. It was prefixed and $75 each. As soon as we got seated, I noticed there were a lot of empty tables which is weird since it's Christmas. Two biscuits with honey and bacon butter were served before meal and it was amazing. My husband is not a butter person, but he kept wanting more and more of the butter.  As appetizers we got scallops and oxtail. It was thin sliced raw scalloped with little chopped pieces of granny smith apple. I guess they wanted it to be like salad- ish. But the citrus flavor was too much like vinegar amd overwhelming. The oxtail was like a square shaped pressed meat served with bread. It was okay, but again it was a bit weird to eat it cold and too blend.  So we had four different entrees; taglioni, oysters, pork belly with rice and beans, and green beans. Pasta was undercooked than al dante and kinda salty. The sauce was supposedly three different stuff mixed I guess? It was kale, mushroom, and butternut, and all I tasted was some heavy salty brown greenish sauce. Green beans were pretty good. It was crunchy and blended well with spicy tomatoes. Pork belly with rice and beans.. The pork belly was slighty crispy outside, moist and tender inside. I think I tasted funnel seeds in the rice and beans and that was all I could taste. The last one, baked oysters... I'm pretty sure the ingredients used in this place is super fresh. But why do you have to put so much lemon juice in every seafood dish, especially when it's cooked??? And the oysters were overcooked and rubbery. Since my husband and I are not huge fan of cinnamon and ginger cake, we decided to go with eggnog panna cotta with cranberry sorbet. It was okay, nothing special.  I trusted these yelp reviews and these famous chefs! But it's huge no no no! The combination was not good, seafood dishes were literally soaked in lemon juice, undercooked pasta, and overcooked rubbery oysters, flavorless, AND it's 75 per person, plus still charge me for a cup of cappuccino?? Seriously??? We got freaking ripped off! I wouldn't even pay 40 bucks for this dinner. I rather go to olive garden or outback! So far I've been to restaurant august, sobou, commander's palace, Doris metropolitan, and Atchafalaya in NOLA. I spend much less than 75 per person for food and it was whole lot better.  I don't think this place even deserve 2 stars, but it's for the biscuits and butter. At least we enjoyed the complimentary bread which is the only thing we enjoyed from our entire dinner. Thanks for ruining my Christmas dinner in NOLA.  I DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS PLACE AT ALL AND WILL NEVER RETURN.

My favorite part is the bar. They have incredible bartenders who like to talk to you, recommend new drinks, as well as ensure you are never short on beverages. The bar menu is an incredible deal and the parmesan french fries are to die for! A great place for college kids on a budget with the bar menu and an amazing place to grab a bite with friends or family

text: I'm surprised there is not more reviews on here for Michael.  He was amazing! The entire experience was very pampering.  He listens to exactly what you want.  The first time I went to the salon I got corrective color.  I can not say enough about how I unhappy I was with my look and when I left I felt amazing.  He really did a perfect job and my hair looked fabulous which I was very skeptical that it would.  I am a "hair snob" and loved the experience and studio.  He has done my cut perfect every time.  He is upfront with what will turn out and what may not so there is no surprises.  He really wants your hair to look fabulous and it has every time.  You can tell all of his experience throughout the cut and color process.   He is extremely professional and creates an wonderful ambiance.  The studio is in a great location and easy to find. I have already recommended him to my co workers and would to anyone else I know.  I couldn't imagine anyone being unhappy with him.

text: The Shawermas here are fantastic.  Great seasoned Shawermas meat, tasty feta and cucumber sauce too served on very fresh lebenese bread. And only $5 for a very big portion. Also try the chicken with rice kabsa or the kebab sandwich. Everything made to order and worth the wait. The best Mediterranean food in town! The cafe is housed in a gas station complex and very unassuming but clean and neat. Hand printed menus stuck to the wall add to the charm. As authentic as it gets and the owner is very friendly.

text: What negative thing can I say? Absolutely nothing! We walked right in and was seated right away! Anthony was simply amazing! He explained the menu very well and made us feel as family! The apple old fashion was simply heavenly! We had the steak and pork chop with twice baked potatoes. The steak melted in your mouth and the pork chop was out of this world! I can't say enough how awesome this place is! We definitely will be back!

text: 1.5 stars for service, 2.5 stars for the food.  Stopped in with my friend on the way back from Atlantic City. Wanted to experience a Jersey diner and I suppose this served it's purpose.  The service was pretty dreadful. We waited a good 10 minutes for the waitress to even come over and get our drink orders and another 10 to actually bring out the drinks.  She dropped them off and ran off before we could tell her we were ready to place our order.  Another 10 minutes go by and she finally comes back to take our order.   The food itself didn't take long to come out and was decent diner fare...we both got breakfast foods.  They get a little credit for having scrapple, which was cooked perfectly. Of course the waitress didn't check on us once and it took forever to get the check.   Doubt I'll ever be back in that area again, so no need to worry about going back.
